title: Shey Monastery
tagline: Shey Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Shey Monastery or Gompa or the Shey Palace are complex structures located on a hillock in Shey, 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) to the south of Leh in Ladakh, northern India on the Leh-Mana
overview.description: Shey Monastery or Gompa or the Shey Palace are complex structures located on a hillock in Shey, 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) to the south of Leh in Ladakh, northern India on the Leh-Manali road. Shey was the summer capital of Ladakh in the past. It contains a huge Shakyamuni Buddha statue. It is the second largest Buddha statue in Ladakh.

topPlaces.name: Shey
topPlaces.description: Shey is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, 15 km from Leh towards Hemis.

topPlaces.name: Thikse Monastery
topPlaces.description: Thiksey Monastery or Thiksey Gompa (also transliterated from Ladakhi as Thikse, Thiksay or Tikse) is a Buddhist monastery affiliated with the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism. It is located on top of a hill in Thiksey approximately 19 kilometres (12 mi) east of Leh, in the Ladakh region of northern India.

topPlaces.name: Thiksey
topPlaces.description: Thiksey is a village and the headquarters of its eponymous block in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Chuchot Gongma
topPlaces.description: Chuchot Gongma is a village-group and the headquarter of Chuchot block in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Saboo, Leh
topPlaces.description: Saboo is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Choglamsar
topPlaces.description: Choglamsar, also spelt Chuglamsar, is a census town in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located on the bank of the Indus River.

topPlaces.name: Stok
topPlaces.description: Stok is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, in the Indus Valley 17 km southeast of the Leh town.

topPlaces.name: Matho Monastery
topPlaces.description: Matho Monastery, or Matho Gonpa or Mangtro Monastery or Mangtro Gonpa, from the Tibetan "mang" that means "many" and "tro" that means "happiness", is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery located 26 kilometres southeast of Leh in Ladakh, northern India, on the banks of the Indus River. The village of Matho is located at the mouth of a deep gorge running out of the Zanskar Range and across the Indus.

items.note: Shey is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, 15 km from Leh towards Hemis.

Thiksey Monastery or Thiksey Gompa (also transliterated from Ladakhi as Thikse, Thiksay or Tikse) is a Buddhist monastery affiliated with the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism. It is located on top of a hill in Thiksey approximately 19 kilometres (12 mi) east of Leh, in the Ladakh region of northern India.
Thiksey is a village and the headquarters of its eponymous block in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.
Chuchot Gongma is a village-group and the headquarter of Chuchot block in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.
Saboo is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.
Choglamsar, also spelt Chuglamsar, is a census town in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located on the bank of the Indus River.
Stok is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, in the Indus Valley 17 km southeast of the Leh town.

Matho Monastery, or Matho Gonpa or Mangtro Monastery or Mangtro Gonpa, from the Tibetan "mang" that means "many" and "tro" that means "happiness", is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery located 26 kilometres southeast of Leh in Ladakh, northern India, on the banks of the Indus River. The village of Matho is located at the mouth of a deep gorge running out of the Zanskar Range and across the Indus.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shey Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shey Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shey Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (11 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (277 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shey Monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shey Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shey Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Shey Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shey Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Shey, Thikse Monastery, Thiksey, Chuchot Gongma, Saboo, Leh.
